episodes.title: My Sister's Take Episode 4:  Our truth about Trina Kamp the Cult Leader.
episodes.description:
To the members of the Church of Immortal Consciousness, Trina Kamp was the spiritual leader at the center of the group.
In this episode, we take a closer look at the woman who shaped the culture, beliefs, and daily life of the community we were born into. Trina co-founded the Church of Immortal Consciousness and became known for her claims of channeling- or “trancing”-a spiritual entity called Dr. Pahlvon Duran. Through these trance sessions and teachings, she became the central authority figure within the group.
Although Trina was technically our grandmother, that relationship was often overshadowed by the role she played within the church. Our experience of her was not primarily as a grandmother, but as the woman around whom everything revolved.
In this conversation, we share our memories of growing up in her orbit and offer a behind-the-scenes look at the person behind the teachings, the trance sessions, and the public image. We discuss how her influence shaped the community, affected those closest to her, and created dynamics that extended far beyond a typical family relationship.
This is not a biography of Trina Kamp. It’s a personal account of what it was like to grow up near the center of her world.

Subscribe to hear more of about our cult life.

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCsQ5DyH2qO7nojvTMFdxzog?sub_confirmation=1

Before we dive in — a few things to keep in mind. My Sisters Take is a personal storytelling podcast. Everything we share is based on our own lived experiences growing up in a high-control group. We're not doctors, therapists, or lawyers — just two sisters finally telling our story. Some episodes touch on heavy topics like religious trauma, spiritual abuse, physical abuse and cult life, so please check in with yourself as you listen. Your mental health matters more than any episode. If something we share brings up difficult emotions, please reach out to a qualified mental health professional.

My Sisters Take provides a platform for hosts and guests to share their personal experiences and opinions. The views expressed are solely those of the individual speaking and do not necessarily reflect the views of the podcast, its hosts, or producers. Any allegations or claims discussed are based on personal accounts and have not been independently verified. All individuals mentioned are presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law. My Sisters Take assumes no legal responsibility for statements made by guests or hosts during any episode.

episodes.title: My Sisters Take Episode 3: Grandfather Figures and Cult Leaders
episodes.description:
In this episode of My Sister’s Take, sisters Sefa and Rose discuss Steven Kamp, the founder and leader of the Church of Immortal Consciousness (COIC), the religious cult where they were born and raised.

While Trina Kamp was known as the spiritual voice of the organization, Steven Kamp was the architect of the community itself- shaping its structure, culture, hierarchy, and day-to-day life. As both the leader of the organization and their grandfather, his influence extended far beyond the community and deeply impacted their family and childhood.

In this conversation, Sefa and Rose share their memories, observations, and personal experiences of Steven Kamp, exploring the role he played within the cult and the lasting effects of the system he created.

🎙️ My Sister’s Take is a podcast where sisters Sefa and Rose share honest conversations about growing up in a cult, healing from religious trauma, family dynamics, and rebuilding life beyond the world they were born into.

Subscribe to hear more of about our cult life.

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCsQ5DyH2qO7nojvTMFdxzog?sub_confirmation=1

episodes.title: My Sisters Take Episode 2: Foundations: Our Cult Life
episodes.description:
Episode 2: A Bird's Eye View of the Cult - Beliefs, Structure & Daily Cult Life

In this episode, we give a broad overview of the religious cult we were raised in to help set context for future conversations.

We explain the core beliefs, teaching philosophies, and the structure that shaped everyday life inside the group. This episode is meant to give listeners a foundational understanding of how the system worked and what cult life was like within it.

This context will  help ground the personal stories we share in later episodes.

Subscribe to hear more of about our cult life.

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCsQ5DyH2qO7nojvTMFdxzog?sub_confirmation=1

episodes.title: My Sisters Take Episode 1: Everybody Loves A Cult Story -  Our Cult Expose
episodes.description:
Episode 1: Introduction- Our Story and Why We-re Starting This Podcast

In this first episode, we introduce ourselves and share why we decided to start the podcast. Our cult expose.

We are sisters who were born into and raised in a religious cult. We talk about who we are today, what that experience was like growing up, and how it has shaped our lives since leaving.

We also share why now is the time to speak publicly about our story, and what we hope this podcast will explore in future episodes - including personal experiences, dipper context, and reflections on life inside and after a high-control environment.

This is the beginning of our story.

Subscribe to hear more of about our cult life.

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCsQ5DyH2qO7nojvTMFdxzog?sub_confirmation=1
